commit.message:
Fix false positives in exploit/scan modules (FW3.7.1)

- Add _discover_params(): crawl real page params before testing
- SQLi: use baseline comparison to filter pre-existing SQL-like text
- SQLi: time-based threshold now requires +4s vs baseline (not raw 2.5s)
- XSS: use unique session marker instead of generic payloads
- XSS: remove DOM sink false positives (innerHTML/eval exist everywhere)
- CMDi: use echo CMDI_CONFIRMED marker, remove generic 'ttl' indicator
- LFI: remove empty indicator payload (matched everything)
- LFI: use specific indicators (root:x:0:0:, HTTP_HOST=)
- vBulletin: require 'vbulletin' in response body (not just 302)
- upload_check: only report 200 with upload form content (not 403)
- sqli_scan: discover real params from page before injecting

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix: run bot and web server in single asyncio event loop for Linux compatibility

python-telegram-bot v20+ requires signal handlers in the main thread, which
caused a crash when the bot ran in a background thread on Railway (Linux).
Refactored run.py to use asyncio.run() with uvicorn.Server.serve() and the
bot running concurrently via async context manager.

Fixes:

- Version strings updated to FW3.7

commit.message:
Fix assessment telemetry bypass: server-side validation + pre-fill prevention

A student exploited the assessment system by pre-filling answers into
lesson_block_responses via DevTools/Firestore, then submitting instantly
with near-zero engagement. The server blindly trusted client-reported
metrics, so the submission passed as NORMAL despite 2s engagement.

Server-side fixes (functions/src/index.ts):
- Flag submissions with <30s engagement but non-empty responses
- Flag submissions with <5s average per response (implausible speed)
- Flag ghost submissions (0 keystrokes + 0 pastes + non-empty answers)
- Validate client-reported engagement against server-computed elapsed time
- Store serverElapsedSec and clientReportedEngagement for audit trail
- Log detailed warning on every FLAGGED assessment submission

Client-side fixes:
- Proctor.tsx: Clear saved block responses on assessment mount (prevents
  pre-fill via Firestore writes before timer starts)
- ResourceViewer.tsx: Require minimum 30s engagement before submit button
  works (UX guard; server enforces independently)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(config): add file locking to prevent concurrent config overwrites (#131)

## Summary
- Concurrent CLI processes could overwrite each other's config changes
(e.g., losing auth token during auto-update check)
- Add `fs2` file locking and an atomic `update_config` helper that holds
an exclusive lock during the entire read-modify-write cycle
- Migrate `store_token`, `clear_credentials`, and
`record_update_check_now` to use `update_config`

Fixes #128

## Detail Bug
bug_0f92f118-1405-4768-92a6-630060dc3170

## Test plan
- [x] Run two CLI commands concurrently that modify different config
fields and verify both changes persist
- [x] Verify `detail auth login` still stores token correctly
- [x] Verify auto-update check timestamp is still recorded

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add Python autoCalibrate2 port with track matching and batch validation

Port of autoCalibrate2.m and compare_tracks2.m from BirdMigration
ThruTrackerScripts to Python, enabling fully automated calibration
from ThruTracker batch_3D_data.mat files without MATLAB.

- api/compare_tracks.py: Track comparison between cameras using
  flight-direction correlation, circular correlation coefficient,
  and frame offset detection
- api/auto_calibrate.py: Full auto-calibration pipeline including
  batch data loading, DBSCAN-based match filtering, background
  point assembly, easyWand calibration, and outlier removal
- scripts/validate_calibration.py: Validation script comparing
  Python results against MATLAB calibration.mat files

Validated on 34/37 CA_2025 per-night calibrations with DLT RMSE
within ~4% of MATLAB on reference nights (Sep-24, Oct-05).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix thread-safety, path traversal, and code quality issues

- Refactor _run_ocr_scan_manual() and _run_watcher_check() to be pure
  functions; all game_state mutations now happen under the asyncio lock
- Add path traversal protection to screenshot serving endpoint
- Add 30s rate limiting to /api/bug-report endpoint
- Add startup validation warnings when augment/champion/item data is empty
- Expose data_loaded, augments_loaded, items_loaded in /health endpoint
- Replace deprecated datetime.utcnow() with datetime.now(timezone.utc)
- Remove dist2/ build artifacts from git tracking (~64k lines of binaries)
- Add dist2/ and build2/ to .gitignore
- Add clarifying comments for movement_speed mapping and ARAM map filter

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(infra): Docker deployment for Raspberry Pi (Phase 6) (#93)

* feat(infra): add Docker containerization and CI workflow (MGG-52, MGG-54, MGG-55, MGG-57)

Multi-stage Dockerfile combining .NET API and React SPA into a single
image. Docker Compose with PostgreSQL 17 (Pi-tuned), security constraints,
and named volume. GitHub Actions workflow for multi-arch builds (amd64/arm64)
with GHCR publishing and Trivy scanning. Conditional HTTPS redirect and
SPA static file serving in production.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(infra): add production operations tooling (MGG-58, MGG-59, MGG-61, MGG-62, MGG-63, MGG-64)

Environment template, secret generation, volume inspection, backup/restore,
update/rollback scripts. Health check now verifies DB connectivity.
Response compression with Brotli and Gzip. .gitignore excludes .env files.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(api): add security hardening and deployment docs (MGG-65, MGG-70, MGG-60)

Forwarded headers for reverse proxy (Nginx Proxy Manager). Rate limiting
with sliding window (100 req/min global) and fixed window (5 req/min auth).
Deployment guide, fail2ban config, and security checklist documentation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remediate 6 bugs found in PR #80 container architecture review

- Remove AppHost from .dockerignore to fix COPY failure (BUG-001)
- Make auth rate limiter per-IP instead of global (BUG-002)
- Use build digest for Trivy scan instead of SHA tag (BUG-003)
- Return 503 from health endpoint when database is unreachable (BUG-004)
- Add EXIT trap in restore.sh to restart app on failure (BUG-005)
- Trust Docker bridge network in forwarded headers config (BUG-006)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remediate 3 additional bugs from PR #80 adversarial review

- Swap UseRouting() before UseRateLimiter() so rate limiting applies to routed endpoints
- Map Docker TARGETARCH to dotnet -a arch (amd64→x64) for cross-platform builds
- Source .env in backup.sh and restore.sh so env vars are available outside compose

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remediate 5 remaining bugs from PR #80 adversarial review

- Add .env sourcing to update.sh and rollback.sh for APP_PORT resolution
- Capture image ID (not just tag) before pull for reliable rollback
- Add --single-transaction to restore.sh psql for atomic restores
- Add default case to Dockerfile architecture switch to fail on unsupported arch

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: correct OpenAPI spec path in Dockerfile and update HealthController tests

The Dockerfile copied the OpenAPI spec to /app/openapi/spec.yaml but the
client's generate:types script resolves ../../openapi/spec.yaml from
/app/client/ to /openapi/spec.yaml. Also adds HealthControllerTests that
match the updated Get(dbContext, cancellationToken) signature.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(docker): pass target architecture to dotnet restore

The restore step didn't include the runtime identifier, but publish used
-a ${DOTNET_ARCH} which requires linux-x64/linux-arm64 in the restore
graph. Pass -a to restore so the assets file includes the correct target.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(docker): restore with runtime ID and ReadyToRun

- Use -r linux-${DOTNET_ARCH} and -p:PublishReadyToRun=true on restore
  so runtime packs are available for --no-restore publish

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ci: skip arm64 build on pull requests

The arm64 QEMU build adds ~18 min of CI time but PR builds never push
images. Only build arm64 on pushes to main and tag events.

Resolves MGG-245

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf(infra): switch GHA cache export from mode=max to mode=min (#91)

mode=max exports all intermediate layers to GitHub Actions cache,
which took ~10.6 min in recent runs. mode=min only caches final
image layers, saving 5-10 minutes per build. The Dockerfile layers
don't change frequently, so caching only the final layers is sufficient.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf(infra): native arm64 Docker CI builds (MGG-242) (#92)

* perf(infra): replace QEMU emulation with native arm64 runners (MGG-242)

Split Docker Publish workflow into three jobs: build (matrix with native
runners per arch), merge (multi-arch manifest), and scan (Trivy). This
eliminates ~18 min of QEMU emulation overhead by using GitHub's native
ubuntu-latest-arm64 runners for arm64 builds, running both architectures
in parallel.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(infra): resolve matrix context and Trivy image-ref bugs

- Move arm64 PR skip from job-level `if` (where matrix context is
  unavailable) to step-level condition on the build step
- Fix Trivy image-ref to use metadata-action's single-value `version`
  output instead of multi-line `tags` output

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): exclude arm64 Docker build from PR matrix

The arm64 job was stuck in pending on PRs because it requested
ubuntu-latest-arm64 runner but all build steps were skipped.
Use a matrix-prep job to dynamically exclude arm64 from the matrix
on pull requests, so only amd64 is built for validation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(scripts): fix rollback double-tag, relative backup path, and retention off-by-one

- Strip existing tag from COMPOSE_IMAGE before re-tagging to prevent
  invalid references like ghcr.io/repo:latest:latest during rollback
- Use SCRIPT_DIR for backup.sh invocation so update.sh works from any CWD
- Use RETENTION_DAYS - 1 in find -mtime to match declared 7-day policy

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ci: add type-drift check to frontend CI job

The Docker build regenerates client types from the live OpenAPI spec,
but CI never did — allowing types to silently drift. Add a build step
that regenerates types and runs tsc before tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

local_density.py; two docstring fixes in base.py and wildland.py).
